definitions: To prod or jab with a finger or a pointed object.
To push or thrust something in a particular direction.
To make a hole or opening by pushing.
To appear or stick out from behind or under something.
examples: She likes to poke fun at her brother.
He used a stick to poke the fire.
A rabbit poked its head out of the burrow.
Don't poke your nose into my business.
The child poked at the cake with a fork.
